We recently enabled PRD from our TEST instance as we are trying to create our first ever custom report in LMS. I created the query already in Plateau and when Previewing Results it works perfectly fine.

Now, when I tried to import and run the report I encounter FAILED status with an issue Could not find table/view PA_STUDENT in schema SC44LP50_STAGE_BOOT: line 32 col 5 (at pos 1270) .

Please advise what went wrong, upon checking the Plateau I dont see schema SC44LP50.

Anyone encountered same issue with me? Can please help advise? Thank you!

 

Regards,

Sally

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service with camera
entry.content:
Introduction

Uploading files is a common requirement in various business scenarios. As SAP Build Apps developer, we are often required to add file upload capability into our apps to fulfill business requirements and enhance user experience.

One of such use case is to upload documents to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service to process large business documents that have content in headers and tables. You can use the extracted information, for example, to automatically process payables, invoices, or payment notes and make sure that invoices and payables match.

Its Upload Document API requires you to sent a POST request with "multipart/form-data" payload.

In this blog, I will show how you can make use of the new "HTTP Destination Request" flow function along with "Take photo" flow function to achieve that.

By the end of this blog, you will learn how to:

  1. Set up your DOX service destination to work with Build Apps.
  2. Install your destination to be used in Build Apps and test the connection to your destination in Build Apps.
  3. Use "Take photo" to let user to capture photo with device and pass the resulting image to "HTTP destination request"
  4. Use "HTTP destination request" to upload the image to your DOX service API via the installed destination.

Pre-requisites

  1. You must have SAP Build Apps already subscribed in your subaccount.
  2. You must have SAP Document Information Extraction Service subscribed.
  3. All required roles for SAP Builds Apps and Document Information Extraction Service are assigned accordingly to your user.

Setting up Destination for Document Information Extraction Service

If you already have a working destination to your DOX service, you can use it or clone it while referring to the steps below to make it compatible with the examples in this blog:

  1. Go to SAP BTP cockpit and create a destination pointing to your DOX instance.
    • For this example, I recommend that the URL of the destination is pointing to the root of your DOX service instance:
      • Example: https://aiservices-dox.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/document-information-extraction/v1/
      • We will append additional relative path to your destination later when using the "HTTP Destination Request" flow function.
    • For authentication related configurations, you can refers to your DOX instance's service key file. These configuration are inside the "uaa" section of the file.
      • TIPS: Token Service URL typically ends with /oauth/token. The UAA URL you copied from the service key usually doesn't contain that. So, you must append /oauth/token yourself when entering the URL to the Token Service URL field.
    • Add the required additional properties in the destination for Build Apps' usage:
      • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
      • BuildApps.Enabled: true
      • BuildApps.ApiType: rest
      • MobileEnabled: true
    • These are the minimal properties required for Build Apps, you may add other properties for other purposes too. Example:

      Hint: "Check Connection" in the BTP Destination cockpit only tells you that the URL is reachable, it does not indicate that the authentication is set up correctly. So, you should not rely solely on it to tell you that your destination is configured correctly.

Install DOX Service Destination in Your Build Apps Project

  1. Open your Build Apps project.
  2. Go to “Integrations” tab.
    1. Press on “Add Integration” button.
    2. Press on the “BTP DESTINATIONS” button in the SAP Systems card.
    3. Search for your DOX destination and select it. It should show a screen with “The destination was recognised as REST api” message.

      Note:
      • If you can't find your destination, do check that you have added the BuildApps.Enabled=true property in your destination.
      • If you don't see that message,do check that you have added the BuildApps.ApiType=rest property in your destination.
    4. Press "INSTALL INTEGRATION" button to install the destination.
    5. (Optional) Testing the connection to destination using Entity's list operation
      1. You can test the connection to your DOX destination by creating adding a REST API data entity and use it to call one of the GET API available in your service, assuming you have the right access. Otherwise, you can skip this steps or choose different API to test with.
      2. Press on the "Add REST API Data Entity" button. You should get a dialog pop up.
      3. In the dialog, enter the following information:
        • Name: Documents
        • Autogenerate configuration: enabled
        • Entity path within the API: /document/jobs
        • Enable Capabilities: enable "list" and uncheck the rest of them
        • Press "Add" button
      4. You should now see the DocumentJobs entity and its Fields and more.
        If you are seeing error here and it couldn't generate any Fields for you, then it's likely there's something wrong with your path, or destination configuration. Make sure to check your destination's URL and authentication configuration.
      5. You can use "Browse Real Data" button to check if you can see expected data entries and no errors.

      6. If you are not seeing any errors, it means the connection to your destination is good for usage.
    6. You can now Exit this page.

Using HTTP Destination Request to Upload File

  1. Go to “User Interface” tab.
  2. Press on the button (or any other UI control) that you wish to use to trigger the file upload and then press on the "Add logic to <your control>" at the bottom of the screen. This should show the Logic Canvas.
  3. Press the “Marketplace” button under the "Core" tab on the left panel.
    1. Search for “HTTP destination request” flow function and install it.
    2. After it is installed you should see it in the "INSTALLED" tab of the Logic Canvas.
  4. You are now ready to use “HTTP destination request” to upload file:
    1. From the "CORE" tab, scroll down to the "Device" category and drag “Take Photos” flow function to the logic flow canvas.
      ℹ️ Note: "Take photo" flow function is currently only available when you run your app in mobile device. It will do nothing when triggered as web app.
    2. Link the button's “Event: Component tap” node to the “Take photo” node.
    3. Go to the "INSTALLED" tab and drag “HTTP destination request” to the logic flow canvas.
    4. Link “Take photo” node’s output port 1 to the “HTTP destination request” node, because we want to pass the photo taken by user to the “HTTP destination request” node.
    5. Click on the “HTTP destination request” node and on its property panel.
      1. In the Destination dropdown select your installed destination.
      2. Set the “HTTP method” as “POST”.
      3. Set the “Request body type” as “multipart/form-data”.
      4. Set the “Request body” and add entries for each part needed for the target API. In this case, we will be using the SAP Document Information Extraction Upload Document API which requires 2 properties:
        KeyValue
        optionsBind this value to formula using the following sample: ENCODE_JSON({"schemaName":"SAP_invoice_schema","clientId": "default","documentType":"invoice","receivedDate":"2024-12-01"})

        Note: In real use cases, you can use variable to store these information instead, but you must always use ENCODE_JSON to convert them to JSON encoded text
        file

        Bind this value to formula using the following sample: MERGE([outputs["Take photo"].photoFile,{"mimeType":"image/jpeg"}])

        You will see some errors in the formula editor about unassignable object, you can safely ignore that.

        Note: This formula is needed due to an existing issue with missing mime-type in the Take photo output. Once the issue is fixed in the near future, you can simply bind this value to "Output of another node" > "Take photo / Photo file".


        Value for "options" key:

        Value for "file" key:

        Resulting list of key-values for the "Request body":
      5. Expand the Optional Inputs section of the "HTTP destination request":
        In "Path" set "/document/jobs" because that's the path to the API for uploading document to start an information extraction job. The path specified here will be appended to your destination in the final request.
      6. Your "HTTP destination request" is now ready.
    6. Drag and drop 1 "Toast" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output port 1 of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      For Toast linked to port 1 you can bind its message to formula with the following:
      "Result: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].successStatusText
    7. Drag and drop 2 "Alert" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output ports of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      1. For the Alert linked to port 2, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error from backend: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].failureStatusText
      2. For the Alert linked to port 3, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].error
    8. The logic canvas should looks like this:
    9. Save your project and you are ready to preview your app and test the file upload.

Preview and Trigger File Upload

"Take photo" currently only works in iOS or Android platform, therefore we will do the preview on your mobile devices.

  • On your mobile device, install the "SAP Build Apps Preview" app from Apple App Store or Google Play Store.
  • After the app is installed, launch the app on your mobile device and press the center card to create a login PIN code. You should see a temporary PIN code generated.
  • Go back to your Build Apps project in the browser and press on the "Preview" button
  • In the "Preview on your device" section, enter your temporary PIN code into the text box and press "Confirm pin". You should get "Mobile preview authenticated" message if it works.
  • Go back to the"SAP Build Apps Preview" app on your mobile device, you should see list of your apps that can be previewed.
  • Locate and open your app.
  • When the app is loaded, press the file upload button:
  • That should prompt you for permission to take photo (if this is the first time), approve it and take a photo of an invoice.
  • Once the photo is taken the upload via "HTTP destination request" should happen and you should get a result soon.
  • After successful upload you should get "Result: Created" toast:
  • If you check the Document Information Extraction web app, you should see your photo is now uploaded and queued for extraction.
  • Once the extraction is completed you should see be able to see it's result.

Summary

As summary, in this blog, you have learned how to set up your Build Apps project to make use of the "HTTP destination request" and "Take photo" to upload file using multipart/form-data payload.

If you are interested with uploading file to other services such as SAP Document Management Service using "Pick files" and "HTTP destination request" in SAP Build Apps, you can visit this blog: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Management Service (DMS) repository with file picker

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Management Service (DMS) repository with file picker
entry.content:
Introduction

Uploading files is a common requirement in various business scenarios. As SAP Build Apps developer, we are often required to add file upload capability into our apps to fulfill business requirements and enhance user experience.

One of such use case is to upload documents to SAP Document Management Service (DMS) for storage purposes.

DMS Create Document API requires you to sent a POST request with "multipart/form-data" payload.

In this blog, I will show how you can make use of the new "HTTP Destination Request" flow function to achieve that.

By the end of this blog, you will learn how to:

  1. Set up your DMS repository destination to work with Build Apps.
  2. Install your destination to be used in Build Apps and test the connection to your destination in Build Apps.
  3. Use "Take photo" to let user to capture photo with device and pass the resulting image to "HTTP destination request"
  4. Use "HTTP destination request" to upload the image to your DMS repository via the installed destination.

Pre-requisites

  1. You must have SAP Build Apps already subscribed in your subaccount.
  2. You must have SAP Document Management Service (DMS) subscribed with access to their API service.
  3. All required roles for SAP Builds Apps and Document Management Service are assigned accordingly to your user.

Setting up Destination for Document Management Service

If you already have a working destination to your DMS service, you can use it or clone it while referring to the steps below to make it compatible with the examples in this blog:

  1. Created a destination in SAP BTP pointing to your DMS instance.
    • For this example, I recommend that the URL of the destination is pointing to the /root API of your DMS instance:
      • Example: https://api-sdm-xyz.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/browser/1234abcd-56ef-78ab-90cd-1234abcd567/root
      • You can always append additional relative path to your destination later when using the "HTTP Destination Request" flow function.
    • Add the required additional properties in the destination for Build Apps' usage:
      • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
      • BuildApps.Enabled: true
      • BuildApps.ApiType: rest
      • MobileEnabled: true
    • These are the minimal properties required for Build Apps, you may add other properties for other purposes too. For example:

      Hint: "Check Connection" in the BTP Destination cockpit only tells you that the URL is reachable, it does not indicate that the authentication is set up correctly. So, you should not rely solely on it to tell you that your destination is configured correctly.

Installing DMS Destination in Your Build Apps Project

  1. Open your Build Apps project.
  2. Go to “Integrations” tab.
    1. Press on “Add Integration” button.
    2. Press on the “BTP DESTINATIONS” button in the SAP Systems card.
    3. Search for your DMS repository destination and select it. It should show a screen with “The destination was recognised as REST api” message.

      Note:
      • If you can't find your destination, do check that you have added the BuildApps.Enabled=true property in your destination.
      • If you don't see that message,do check that you have added the BuildApps.ApiType=rest property in your destination.
    4. Press "INSTALL INTEGRATION" button to install the destination.
    5. (Optional) Testing the connection to destination using Entity's list operation
      1. You can test the connection to your DOX destination by creating adding a REST API data entity and use it to call one of the GET API available in your service, assuming you have the right access. Otherwise, you can skip this steps or choose different API to test with.
      2. Press on the "Add REST API Data Entity" button. You should get a dialog pop up.
      3. In the dialog, enter the following information:
        • Name: Documents
        • Autogenerate configuration: enabled
        • Entity path within the API: /?cmisselector=children
        • Enable Capabilities: enable "list" and uncheck the rest of them
        • Press "Add" button
      4. You might see a warning about some troubles with the resource schema. You can safely ignore that as we are only using this as verification.
      5. You should now see the Documents entity and its Fields and more.
        If you are seeing other error here and it couldn't generate any Fields for you, then it's likely there's something wrong with your path, or destination configuration. Make sure to check your destination's URL and authentication configuration.
      6. You can use "Browse Real Data" button to check if you can see expected data entries and no errors.
      7. If you are not seeing any errors, it means the connection to your destination is good for usage.
    6. You can now Exit this page.

Using HTTP Destination Request to Upload File

  1. Go to “User Interface” tab.
  2. Press on the button (or any other UI control) that you wish to use to trigger the file upload and then press on the "Add logic to <your control>" at the bottom of the screen. This should show the Logic Canvas.
  3. Press the “Marketplace” button under the "Core" tab on the left panel of the Logic Canvas.
    1. Search for “Pick files” flow function and install it and then re-open the Marketplace again.
    2. Search for “HTTP Destination Request” flow function and install it.
    3. After both are installed you should see them in the "INSTALLED" tab of the Logic Canvas.
  4. You are now ready to use “HTTP destination request” to upload file:
    1. Go to the "INSTALLED" tab of the Logic Canvas.
    2. Drag “Pick files” to the logic flow canvas.
    3. Link the button's “Event: Component tap” node to the “Pick files” node.
    4. Drag “HTTP destination request” to the logic flow canvas.
    5. Link “Pick files” node’s output port 1 to the “HTTP destination request” node, because we want to pass the files selected by user to the “HTTP destination request” node.
    6. Click on the “HTTP destination request” node and on its property panel.
      1. In the Destination dropdown select your installed destination.
      2. Set the “HTTP method” as “POST”.
      3. Set the “Request body type” as “multipart/form-data”.
      4. Set the “Request body” and add entries for each part needed for the target API. In this case, we will be using the DMS Create Document API which requires 10 properties:
        KeyValue
        cmisactioncreateDocument
        propertyId[0]cmis:name
        propertyValue[0]Bind this to formula using the following: outputs[“Pick “files].files[0].name
        propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
        propertyValue[1]cmis:document
        filenamebind this to formula using the following: outputs[“Pick “files].files[0].name
        _charset_UTF-8
        includeAllowableActionstrue
        succinctfalse
        media bind this to formula using the following: outputs[“Pick “files].files[0]
        Note: “Pick files” flow function allows user to pick one or more files. In this scenario, we will only take the first item of the selected files by accessing index 0, hence the files[0]
        For example:
      5. Your "HTTP destination request" is now ready.
      6. ℹ️ Optional Inputs
        In the Optional Inputs section of the"HTTP destination request", you can also specify additional "Path" that’s relative to your destination, if needed. The path specified here will be appended to your destination in the final request. In this example, we don’t need to specify path as our destination is already pointing to the target API path. However, for DMS create document API you may append inner folder path, if you wish, e.g. /FolderOne
        You can also specify additional HTTP "Headers" here.
    7. Drag and drop 1 "Toast" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output port 1 of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      For Toast linked to port 1 you can bind its message to formula with the following:
      "Result: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].successStatusText
    8. Drag and drop 2 "Alert" flow function to the Logic Canvas and link them to each of the output ports of the “HTTP destination request” flow function.
      1. For the Alert linked to port 2, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error from backend: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].failureStatusText
      2. For the Alert linked to port 3, you can bind its message to formula with the following:
        "Error: " + outputs["HTTP destination request"].error
    9. The logic canvas should looks like this:
    10. Save your project and you are ready to preview your app and test the file upload.

Preview and Trigger File Upload

  • Press on the "Preview" button > Open web preview and choose your app.
  • When the app is loaded, press the file upload button:
  • Pick a file from your file system and press Open:
  • After succesful upload you should get "Result: Created" toast:
  • You should see the file now uploaded to your target repository.
  • If possible, check your repository for the uploaded file.

Summary

As summary, in this blog, you have learned how to set up your Build Apps project to make use of the "HTTP destination request" and "Pick files" to upload file using multipart/form-data.

If you are interested with uploading file to other services such as SAP Document Information Extraction Service using "Take photo"and "HTTP destination request" in SAP Build Apps, you can visit this blog: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service repository with camera.

entry.title: Please check the application compatibility with SLES15 SP6.
entry.content:
The current OS is SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4.

The customer wants to know the compatibility between Solution Manager 7.2 with SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP6


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Please check the application compatibility with SLES1...
entry.content:
Dear *,

 

Application instance that are supported on SLES 15, are supported with all new SLES 15 SP versions as well. This is stated in note:

##2578899 - SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15: Installation Note

-------------

Service Packs for SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15

Updates for SLES 15 are released in the form of Service Packs (SP). When you import a Service Pack, many RPM packages are updated to a new version. For SLES 15 you may use all Service Packs officially released by SUSE. It is highly recommended to install the most current Service Pack. Also, kindly notice that after the release of a new Service Pack, older versions of packages that are updated with this Service Pack, are considered obsolete.

-------------

 

Additionally, you also can find out which Operating System (OS) releases as well as which Database System (DB) releases are supported for a planned/intended Solution Manager 7.2 installation in KBA .

##3429213 - How-to determine Operating System and Database System releases compatible to / supported for Solution Manager 7.2 via PAM (Product Availability Matrix)

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2302.1 – Sales Volume - Flexible Analysis
entry.content:
Hi Siva,

The fiori app ‘Sales Volume – Flexible Analysis’ is based on analytical cube view I_BillingDocumentItemCube with most fields from billing document header and item.

I guess the ‘Discount’ field mentioned is measure(KPI) which is in Pricing Element, but the cardinality of billing document item and pricing element discount is 0 to *.

You can build you own custom analytical query and cube base on I_BillingDocumentItem  and I_BillingDocumentItemPrcgElmnt, calculate and expose discount with your own logic.

Best Regards,

Joey

entry.title: How to Check if My System is Affected by SAP Security Patch Day Notes?
entry.content:
Hello Professionals,

I would like to know how to check whether my system is affected by vulnerabilities mentioned in the SAP Security Patch Day. For example:

SAP Note 3469791
[CVE-2024-54198] Information Disclosure Vulnerability through Remote Function Call (RFC) in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP
Product: SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP
Version: KRNL64NUC 7.22, 7.22EXT, KRNL64UC 7.22, 7.22EXT, 7.53, KERNEL 7.22, 7.53, 7.54, 7.77, 7.89, 7.93

Link: SAP Note 3469791

My systems are as follows:

  1. SAP ECC6 EHP6

    • System Information and SM51:

  2. SAP GRC

    • System Information and SM51:

       

  3. SAP Fiori

    • System Information and SM51: 

       

I would like guidance on how to compare my system's software components to the affected versions mentioned in the SAP Security Notes. Specifically, where should I check in the system (e.g., Kernel version, software component details) and how should I interpret the information?

Any advice or step-by-step instructions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP SAP CTRM vs. Other ETRM Systems: A Strategic Comparison
entry.content:
Commodity Trading and Risk Management (CTRM) systems are essential for businesses operating in volatile markets, such as energy and commodities. These systems enable companies to manage their trading operations, risk exposures, and financial processes effectively. This article explores a strategic comparison between SAP CTRM  and other ETRM systems, focusing on key capabilities, challenges, and business impacts.

1. Core Functionalities: A Comparative Overview

FeatureSAP Commodity Management (SAP CTRM)Other ETRM Systems

Deal Capture & PricingRequires customization for advanced deal structuresTypically offers robust out-of-the-box functionality
Risk ManagementRequires medium to high customizationSpecialized tools often provide comprehensive features
SchedulingStandard functionality available with configurationAdvanced scheduling features with built-in flexibility
Inventory ManagementIntegrated with SAP ERP, needs additional configurationDedicated inventory workbench with minimal configuration
Reporting & AnalyticsFunctional but heavily reliant on customizationRich, pre-built analytics tailored for trading operations

2. Customization Requirements

  • SAP CTRM: While SAP CTRM provides a strong foundation, achieving CTRM capabilities requires significant customization and enhancements, particularly for complex trading and risk management needs. This can extend implementation timelines and require substantial investment in development, testing, and training.

  • Other ETRM Systems: Many ETRM systems are purpose-built for commodity trading and come with advanced functionalities out of the box. This reduces customization efforts but may pose challenges in integrating with broader ERP systems.

3. Integration and Ecosystem Alignment

  • SAP CTRM: Offers seamless integration with SAP’s broader ERP ecosystem, making it a strong choice for organizations already using SAP solutions. This enables a unified platform for managing supply chain, finance, and trading operations.

  • Other ETRM Systems: While they excel in trading and risk management, integration with ERP systems often requires additional middleware or custom connectors, potentially increasing complexity.

4. Risk Management Capabilities

Managing financial exposure, market volatility, and regulatory compliance are critical for any CTRM system:

  • SAP CTRM: Requires extensive customization to achieve sophisticated risk monitoring, position management, and exposure reporting capabilities.

  • Other ETRM Systems: Typically deliver robust risk management features with real-time exposure analysis, making them ideal for fast-paced trading environments.

5. Implementation and Change Management

  • SAP CTRM: Transitioning to SAP CM involves significant change management efforts due to high levels of customization and the need to retrain teams. This is especially true when replacing a mature ETRM system.

  • Other ETRM Systems: Implementation is often quicker due to pre-built capabilities, but organizations must manage integration complexities and ensure alignment with existing business processes.

6. Key Business Considerations

AspectSAP CTRMOther ETRM Systems
Total Cost of OwnershipHigher due to customization and extended implementationModerate with quicker time-to-value
ScalabilityHigh, given its integration with the SAP ecosystemModerate, depending on the system’s integration capability
Long-Term ViabilityAligns well with SAP-centric digital transformation goalsBest suited for specialized trading environments

7. Recommendations

For organizations evaluating SAP CTRM against other ETRM systems, here are some key considerations:

  • Business Alignment: If your organization heavily relies on SAP ERP, SAP CTRM offers seamless integration and a unified platform, making it a strategic fit.

  • Functionality vs. Integration: Other ETRM systems may provide superior out-of-the-box trading functionality but could increase complexity when integrating with ERP systems.

  • Customization vs. Specialization: SAP CTRM’s need for customization must be weighed against the specialized features of other ETRM systems to determine the best fit for your business needs.

  • Long-Term Goals: For companies aiming for end-to-end digital transformation, SAP CTRM provides a scalable and sustainable solution, albeit with initial challenges.

8. Conclusion

Choosing between SAP CTRM and other ETRM systems depends on your organization’s specific needs, existing technology stack, and long-term goals. While SAP CTRM aligns well with SAP’s ecosystem, other ETRM systems excel in delivering specialized trading and risk management features. Organizations must carefully assess the trade-offs and prioritize their strategic objectives to make an informed decision.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Major shifts in SAP Fiori implementation
entry.content:
Hi Gopi,

Our recommendation is still to move to embedded. The benefits are not only savings in server costs and simplifying the landscape, it's ongoing savings in cost, time and effort in running your solution and making changes from upgrade to upgrade. 

Unless you are a VERY LARGE customer with VERY special needs, and a HUGE number of consultants to assist, you should seriously move to embedded asap.

Making the move just before an upgrade, or at the start of an upgrade is what most customers seem to do.  It is easiest to transition on the same SAP S/4HANA release which must be within current customer maintenance. 

The longer you wait, the harder it gets, the more you have to do to move. 

If it's a new solution we encourage customer to start on embedded FES from the beginning. 

We cannot possibly guarantee what the future will hold for releases beyond the current or next future release.  This is always subject to change. 

I note that SAP has not changed direction on embedded FES in the last 9 years since 2017.

But my case is slightly different. I am not using the standard curSalary field as base for calculating merit, extra and lumpsum, instead using custom Current Pay Rate (calculated as Current Pay Rate+promotion). So basically merit amount should be custom Current Pay Rate*merit%. 

But when I am trying to update the xml, getting error:

Attribute "fieldId" with value "customSalAfterMinAdjust" must have a value from the list "curSalary promotion merit extra extra2 ".

Also, please can you help me understand what is <merit-calc basedOn="CurrentSalary"></merit-calc> mean, I don’t think any field name called CurrentSalary. I also tried replacing the field name of the custom rate in place of CurrentSalary, but got similar error.

Attribute "basedOn" with value "Current Pay Rate After Bring to Min" must have a value from the list "CurrentSalary Target "

So my question is it possible to use a custom field as base for multiplying merit/extra/lumpsum %?

 

Here is the full xml (lumpsum2 and extra2 are not in use, so has the standard config)

          <lumpsum-calc basedOn="Current Pay Rate After Bring to Min"></lumpsum-calc>

          <lumpsum2-calc basedOn="CurrentSalary"></lumpsum2-calc>

          <merit-calc basedOn="Current Pay Rate After Bring to Min"></merit-calc>

          <comp-compounding useFor="merit">

           <percentage-calc-base-includes fieldId=" customSalAfterMinAdjust "/>

          </comp-compounding>

          </comp-compounding>

            <percentage-calc-base-includes fieldId="promotion"/>

            <percentage-calc-base-includes fieldId="curSalary"/>

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: ASE 16.0 future patch releases

Hi Derek,
There is no statement to say 16.0 SP04 PL07 will be the latest patch version for ASE 16.0.
ASE 16.0 SP03 PL15 is confirmed to be the last PL for SP03, but no such conclusion made for 16.0 SP04x.

I think there should be newer releases after 16.0 SP04 PL07 since the EOMM date for 16.0 is 2027:
ASE 16.0 - End of Mainstream Maintenance: 31 December 2027

Note 3504083 - EoMM Information for SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: CAP LLM plugin vs. AI SDK

Hi @Leoniepopken 

CAP Pluguin is a higher-level abstraction, in the end, AI Core SDK is lower level, fully flexible, and you can integrate it with a CAP application, or any phyton-TS front-end application you require an integration with BTP.

That CAP repo is an example; you take the scenarios there, and you can use them as an inspiration for what can be done, like anonymization of the data before reaching the LLM.

entry.title: Re: configurable products and dynamic pricing
entry.content:

I'm afraid the Commerce Cloud variant product model would not be a good solution to this specific problem.

If the mentioned SAP product variant configuration or the CPQ solution is out of the project scope and you wanted to implement the feature all in Commerce Cloud, you might want to give the OOTB configurable product feature a try. Please refer to the following videos:

An Introduction to Configurable Products - SAP Commerce Cloud

Working with Configurable Products in SAP Commerce Cloud

From there you might want to extend the standard feature by leveraging the underlying configurable product  and PriceFactory service APIs to calculate the final prices based on the product configuration during the checkout flow.

entry.title: Configure Ticket Survey in SAP C4C for Ask HR
entry.content:
In today’s HR service delivery landscape, ensuring employee satisfaction is crucial. With SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) integrated with Ask HR, organizations can streamline ticketing processes and gather feedback on HR interactions through surveys. Ticket surveys provide valuable insights into employee satisfaction and help identify areas of improvement. In this blog, we will walk through the step-by-step process of configuring ticket surveys in SAP C4C for Ask HR.

1- Prerequisites

  • SAP C4C is integrated with your Ask HR solution.
  • The Surveys functionality is enabled in SAP C4C.
  • You have the necessary admin access to make configurations.
  • A survey design tool like Qualtrics (if required) is available for survey creation.

2. Understanding the Survey Workflow in C4C

Ticket surveys in C4C are typically triggered at specific points in the ticket lifecycle, such as ticket closure. The process involves:

  • Creating the survey content.
  • Configuring survey distribution logic.
  • Enabling automation rules to send surveys after ticket resolution.

3. Steps to Configure the Ticket Survey

Step 1: Access a Survey tab in SAP C4C

Go to Administrator > Surveys

 

Step 2: Create a New Survey:

  • Click New and provide a name (e.g., “Ask HR Satisfaction Survey”)

Step 3: Design the Survey:

Click "Design" and add all relevant questions using the correct type.

Step 4: Save and Activate the survey.

4. Configure Survey Distribution

Step 1: Access Survey

Step 2: Go to Business Configuration > Workflow Rules>Create a new rule with all the required details

Business Object and Trigger Condition

Step 3: Define the conditions to trigger a survey

Step 4: Define the survey actions such as Sender Name, Email, Survey Recipients, etc.

Step 5: In the Placeholder Mapping, map #SURVEY2744# to anything, it does not matter
For e.g. you can map it to “Ticket ID” or “External ID” because the code will recognize the placeholder and replace it with a link

Step 6: Create an HTML EMail template, with a placeholder with the format #SURVEYID#Eg. #SURVEY25#, if the ID of the customer satisfaction survey was 25

Sample HTML Code (If the link of the survey received  is not hyperlink, please check the Tags of HTML with attention)

<html>

<head>

<meta content="text/html; charset=utf-8" http-equiv="Content-Type">

</head>

<body>

<p> Hello <p>

<p> Your [Request: #TICKETID#] is complete <p>

<a href=#SURVEY25#>Please complete the survey</a>

</div>

<p> Regards,
PC Support <p>

</body>

</html>

Save with Enconding UTF-8.

Step 7: Review the Workflow rule and activate it.

5. Test the Survey Configuration

  • Create a dummy ticket
  • Change ticket status to closed or solution confirmed based on your workflow condition
  • Open the Email to access the survey link

  • Click on the "Please complete the survey" hyperlink to access the survey and complete the feedback

6. Best Practices

  • Keep Surveys Short: Avoid lengthy surveys to ensure a higher response rate
  • Send Timely Surveys: Trigger surveys immediately after ticket closure to capture fresh feedback.
  • Review Feedback Regularly: Periodic analysis of survey responses helps identify recurring issues.
  • Ensure Anonymity: Assure employees their feedback is confidential to encourage honesty.

Conclusion

Configuring ticket surveys in SAP C4C for Ask HR is a straightforward process that provides significant value by capturing employee feedback. By following the steps outlined above, organizations can better understand employee satisfaction, enhance HR service delivery, and continuously improve the employee experience.

Supporting Resources:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002670262

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002484144

https://me.sap.com/notes/2316200

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blogs-by-sap/no-place-holder-to-create-a-survey-in-workflow-rule-for-tickets-business/ba-p/13217060

entry.title: Re: Multiple developers working on same branch in gCTS in SAP development system
entry.content:
Hi Sunil,

in general, developers contributing to the same project / functionality should work on the same branch to make sure that the whole functionality arrives in the production system at once. If it is separate functionality, then maybe even separate repositories should be used. There is no general rule on how to set up repositories and branches. Only one basic thing that you should always separate customizing and workbench in separate repositories.
If you switch to the main branch, then this switch is done for everyone and new commits of all developers will then go to the main branch. So if this shall not happen, you should inform other developers not to release something while the 'wrong' branch is active. More information on merging strategies is available in here https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4a368c163b08418890a406d413933ba7/de66bedd91ba469dab6f28dc60abcb05.html 

There is no option to use different development branches in one development system in parallel. If this is required in your setup, each developer has to use his own development system. Only one branch of a repository can be active as different branches of a repository contain by nature the same objects and there can only be one active version of an object in an ABAP system at a time. If each developer uses his own system, then they should still use the same repository if they work on the same functionality and then conflict resolution should be used (disable automatic push and pull for the repository) to make sure that no changes of other developers are overwritten.
The intention of branches is to separate different versions of development objects from each other

Kind regards
Karin

entry.title: Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
entry.content:
Abstract

This study guide is designed to assist you in preparing for the SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (P_C4H34_2411). This certification verifies that you possess the proficiency needed to work effectively with the SAP Commerce Cloud Suite. Catering to a professional/expert level audience, this certification demands candidates demonstrate technical skills and a deep understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud functionalities. With three years of practical experience being a prerequisite, the certification addresses complex problem-solving scenarios.

Before we start

To prepare for the certification, it's highly recommended to engage in comprehensive training sessions and/or online learning platforms. The below options provide structured learning paths to develop the necessary skills:

  • Instructor-led Training:
    • C4H340 - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
    • C4H341 - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
  • E-Learning Courses: For those unable to attend training sessions in person, e-learning options are available with equivalent content.
    • Beginner: Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Intermediate: Expand Upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Advanced: Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Hands-on Practice Systems: These trainings and e-learning offerings are complemented by dedicated practice systems, allowing you to perform hands-on exercises with SAP Commerce Cloud. This practical experience is designed to enhance your understanding, with PDFs of the training materials accessible in the exercise environments. Specifically:
    • The following practice systems correspond to the mentioned trainings:
      • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
      • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
    • Additionally, the practice systems related to the three e-learning courses include:
      • Hands-on Practice for Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
      • Hands-on Practice for Expanding Upon SAP Commerce Cloud Technical Essentials
      • Hands-on Practice for Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
         
  • Certification Live Session - SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer Certification Preparation: This live session features an instructor who reviews key exam topics and can directly address many of your related questions.

But please note: These resources provide a comprehensive foundation but aren't exhaustive for examination readiness due to the certification's professional level.

The certification exam is categorized into the 10 topic areas outlined below:

  • Platform Basics
  • Platform Features
  • PCM (Product Content Management)
  • Commerce
  • Customer Experience
  • Order Management and Customer Services
  • Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal
  • Backoffice Framework
  • Integration
  • Managing Clean Core

Let's explore each topic area in detail.

Walkthrough of All Topics

1. Platform Basics (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should focus on the following objectives:

  • Comprehend the Ant build framework and essential configuration files.
  • Explain the concept of extensions and manage them effectively, including property files and dependencies.
  • Describe data modeling concepts and the purpose of 'items.xml', including item types, primary key generation, relationships/collections, enumerations, maps, dynamic attributes, and database type deployment.
  • Manage security aspects related to type-based access rights and restrictions.
  • Master writing flexible search queries.
  • Understand ImpEx syntax, how to execute ImpEx, and its scripting support.
  • Identify the features and implementation of service-oriented architecture and the service layer, including model objects, monitoring via interceptors, and management using 'modelService'.

Important Links:

  • Building SAP Commerce
  • Extension Concept
  • Type System
  • ServiceLayer
  • Users in the SAP Commerce Platform
  • Search restrictions
  • Spring Framework in SAP Commerce
  • Search Mechanisms
  • Data Management with ImpEx
  • ImpEx API

2. Platform Features (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should aim to:

  • Understand the event framework, including special event types like 'ClusterAwareEvent', 'TransactionAwareEvent', and 'AfterSaveEvent'.
  • Manage CronJobs by working with data models, implementation steps, and aborting methods.
  • Identify caching features, such as the framework, eviction policies, configuration options, and clustering.
  • Explain the data conversion and population process.
  • Import data using the system setup utility and patch classes.
  • Understand the implementation and customization of Omni Commerce Connect (OCC).
  • Identify the functionalities of workflows, workflow templates, and the collaboration center.
  • Understand scripting support and its applications.

Important Links:

  • Event System
  • The Cron Job Service
  • Cache
  • Converters and Populators
  • Workflow and Collaboration
  • Omni Commerce Connect
  • Scripting Engine

3. PCM (Product Content Management) (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should aim to understand the following:

  • Product and catalog management, including products, categories, catalog versions, and synchronization management.
  • Different product modeling approaches, such as basic variants, multi-dimensional variants, and the classification system.
  • Media management, focusing on elements like media containers, media formats, and synchronization.

Important Links:

  • Product Modelling
  • Classification
  • Working with Catalogs
  • Synchronizing Catalogs
  • Media
  • Muiti-Dimensional variants

4. Commerce (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the search and navigation module, encompassing backoffice configuration, indexed types, index queries/jobs/operations, indexed properties, facet and facet range configuration, as well as search services and listeners.
  • Identify the features of the adaptive search module, including its configuration, search profiles, and the boosting of items and rules.
  • Manage and customize the rule engine-based promotion module, covering out-of-the-box promotions, conditions and actions, rule-aware objects, and coupon types.
  • Explain the pricing mechanism, focusing on price rows and their decoupling from catalogs.
  • Understand basic stock management, including data models, the stock service, and display on storefronts.
  • Identify cart management and the implementation of the commerce cart service.
  • Recognize the relationship between BaseStore, Catalogs, and Warehouses.

Important Links:

  • Search And Navigation Module
  • Adaptive Search Module
  • Promotion
  • Price, Tax, and Discount Calculation
  • Commerce Service Module

5. Customer Experience (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Identify the out-of-the-box CMS item types, including base store, base site, template, page, content slot, and component, as well as their type hierarchy.
  • Implement and manage custom CMS component types and CMS restrictions.
  • Recognize the key features of SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront.
  • Understand the features and management of CMS content using SmartEdit, including various modes such as preview, basic/advanced edit, versioning, and navigation management.
  • Comprehend the personalization module, including related data models, calculation processes, voters, and commerce customization for personalized search and promotions.

Important Links:

  • SmartEdit
  • WCMS Module
  • Personalization module
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront

6. Order Management and Customer Services (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the basecommerce module and typical Order APIs, including splitting strategies, cancellations, returns, and refunds.
  • Manage existing and custom business processes, focusing on their concepts, node types, and custom implementation.
  • Identify the standard checkout and fulfillment processes.
  • Describe the features of the Assisted Service Module and Customer Service Module.
  • Understand the Order Management Services Module.

Important Links:

  • Base Commerce Module
  • Order Management Services Module
  • Assisted Service Module
  • Customer Service Module
  • Business Process Management

7. Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Explain the architecture of SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • Understand how to set up the code repository.
  • Describe how to configure 'manifest.json' files, manage environments, and configure endpoints.
  • Explain how to connect to the code repository and how to build and deploy your application on the Cloud Portal.

Important Links:

  • Build Manifest Components
  • Cloud Portal
  • Cloud Automation Components
  • Project Customizations

8. Backoffice Framework (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Describe how to create and manage backoffice applications using the Backoffice framework.
    • This includes managing widgets, configuring connections, utilizing the application orchestrator, implementing dynamic forms, and using virtual sockets, among other features.

Important Links:

  • Backoffice Framework Module, especially:
    • Widgets
    • UI Configuration Overview
    • Dynamic Forms in UI Configuration
    • Virtual Sockets
  • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice Framework Developer

9. Integration (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Explain how to apply, manage, and monitor Integration APIs and Webhooks within the Backoffice.
  • Identify the features and functionalities of Cloud Hot Folders.
  • Recognize the basic features of Intelligent Selling Services and the SAP CX AI Toolkit for SAP Commerce Cloud.

Important Links:

  • Integration API Module
  • Cloud Hot Folders
  • Webhooks
  • Intelligent Selling Services
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

10. Managing Clean Core (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the concept of Clean Core and its essential components.
  • Identify the application of Clean Core across various dimensions, including Business Processes, Extensions, Data, Landscapes, and Operations.

Important Links:

  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, especially:
    • Exploring How to Make Landscapes Core Compliant
    • Exploring How to Make Operations Core Compliant

Summary

This study guide sets a strong foundation by outlining crucial topic areas to focus on along with recommended learning resources. Equip yourself with both theoretical knowledge and practical insight to excel in the certification exam.

Happy learning and good luck with your preparation!

entry.title: Error when contingent worker is hired as permanent employee (SF-AD integration)
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing an issue with one of our new hires in the ActiveDirectory-SuccessFactors integration. 

Following message is shown in AD: 

Failure Description

An entry cannot not be provisioned in this target: SuccessFactors. The entry is 12345. The reason the entry cannot be provisioned is internal to that application. An example of why an entry cannot be provisioned is a user with a given public identifier, such as an email address, having previously exercised a regulatory right to be removed from the application.
ReportableIdentifier
emp.emp@company.com

The username of the previous employment was the username AD is trying to update now. emp.emp@company.com

 

The username of the new employment was a number sequence, like 12345. 

 

AD tried to update the new employment with the old username which was not possible since the old username was taken. 

 

From past experience, I have seen that the old username is automatically updated to username_INACT. Why does this not happen in this case? 

 

Hope someone knows the answer to this. I thought that was standard behavior. 

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content: Hello Navi, you stated "I have same issue. Custom entity comes to list but cds views are not. I copied one of the listed CDS views (for example C_BOMMaterialVH to ZC_BOMMaterialVH) and gives C1 contract but still not listed." This is not a bug...currently one can only use Custom Entities as value help views for Key User Custom Fields. You can not use customer created view entities...even if you release them C1 for key users. We are aware of this gap and are already working on that. Best regards Georg

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content:
Hi,

there is an easier way to do this:

  1. Preferences -> Configuration: Logon
  2. select "Expert Mode " On
  3. Press "Save" button
  4. Right-Click on a Node in Logon
  5. From context menu select "Repair"
  6. Dialog opens: "SAP UI Landscape Repair"
  7. Select "Reset includes list"
  8. Press "Execute"

 An you are done.

BR
Robert

entry.title: Re: Output Management in TM - BRF+ or PPF?
entry.content:
Hello @papa261190 

LE-TR (shipments) uses NACE for output processing. In contrast TM relies on PPF actions. 

BRF+ and PPF is not an alternative. They are complementary, they work together. BRF+ can be used to implement conditions for PPF. BRF+ is for PPF, what output conditions are for NACE.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to Retrieve Warehouse Items with Serial Numbers a...
entry.content:
Hi @Fiorella ,

I encountered a similar scenario where I needed to identify the supplier associated with a batch number.

To address this, I created the following view, which I incorporated into my main query to retrieve the supplier details for the batch number.

You can adapt this approach to work with serial numbers as well.

SELECT
DISTINCT T1."ItemCode",
T1."Quantity" AS "Quantity",
T4."DistNumber" AS "LabelBoxNo",
T3."DocNum" AS "ReceivingDocNum",
T3."DocDate" AS "ReceivingDate",
T3."CardCode",
T3."CardName" 
FROM OITL T0 
INNER JOIN ITL1 T1 ON T0."LogEntry" = T1."LogEntry" AND T0."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode" 
INNER JOIN OITM T2 ON T2."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode" 
INNER JOIN OPDN T3 ON T3."DocEntry" = T0."ApplyEntry" AND T3."ObjType" = T0."ApplyType" 
INNER JOIN PDN1 T5 ON T5."DocEntry" = T3."DocEntry" 
INNER JOIN OBTN T4 ON T4."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode" AND T4."AbsEntry" = T1."MdAbsEntry" 
WHERE T0."LocCode" IN ('01','02','03') AND T5."TargetType" <> 21 AND T3."CANCELED" = 'N' 
ORDER BY T1."ItemCode",T4."DistNumber

I hope this provides you with some useful insights!

Thank you.

Manish

entry.title: Re: [Group Reporting] creating subitem categories
entry.content:
Hi,

from a technically point of view it is no problem to add new subitem categories in order to differentiate parts of the balance sheet items. and in former consolidation solution like EC-CS or SEM-BCS it was a kind of best practice in doing so.

But in FIN-CS you've some restrictions especcialy in relation to the transfer of universal journal data and especially the use of correction positings via postins level 0C (for instance you have to set up all subitems to all subitem categories and additionally limit them via max.selections in order to have the entire functionalities for 0C-postings). Furthermore you have to note some dependencies in relation to the behaviour of BCF and CT.

So, from my point of view it is still very beneficial to use different subitems (using the same source: transaction type) to structure and manage postings within the balance sheet. But there are some obstacles so far.

BR
